items.title: Republican Clayton Fuller wins Georgia special election runoff
items.text_preview: Clayton Fuller, a onetime district attorney who campaigned on his loyalty to President Donald Trump, won a special election runoff Tuesday for a House seat in Georgia but by a narrower margin than...
items.summary: Clayton Fuller, a Republican and former district attorney, has won a special election runoff in Georgia's 14th District, defeating Democrat Shawn Harris with 54% of the vote. Fuller, who campaigned on his loyalty to President Donald Trump, will serve out the remainder of the term vacated by former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene. His win allows House Republicans to maintain their narrow majority, at least until the next special election.

items.title: Liberals expand majority in Wisconsin Supreme Court: 3 key takeaways from Tuesday’s election night results
items.text_preview: The Wisconsin Supreme Court expanded its liberal majority to 5-2 on Tuesday following Chris Taylor’s victory over Maria Lazar in the race for an open seat on the high court. Taylor, the...
items.summary: The Wisconsin Supreme Court has expanded its liberal majority to 5-2 after Chris Taylor's victory over Maria Lazar in the statewide election. Taylor, the Democrat-backed liberal, won by more than 20 percentage points, marking the fourth consecutive victory for liberal candidates in the nonpartisan Wisconsin Supreme Court elections. This win solidifies the liberal majority on the court, which has already made significant rulings, including overturning a state abortion ban and ordering new legislative maps.

items.title: US and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire as Trump seizes diplomatic offramp instead of escalation
items.text_preview: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump pulled back on his threats to launch devastating strikes on Iran late Tuesday, swerving to deescalate the war less than two hours before the deadline...
items.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to a 2-week ceasefire, with Iran accepting the terms and agreeing to negotiate with the US in Islamabad starting Friday. As part of the agreement, Iran will allow passage through the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian military management for the next two weeks. The ceasefire comes after US President Donald Trump pulled back on his threats to launch devastating strikes on Iran, opting for a diplomatic approach instead.

items.title: Oil Prices Plummet Dramatically as Stock Futures Soar Following Ceasefire Announcement
items.text_preview: On Tuesday, oil prices experienced a significant decline following Donald Trump’s announcement of a temporary two-week ceasefire with Iran, aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Trump shared the...
items.summary: Oil prices plummeted 18% to below $93 a barrel after US President Donald Trump announced a temporary two-week ceasefire with Iran, aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The ceasefire announcement also led to a surge in stock futures, with Dow futures rising by 1,000 points, and a decrease in Treasury yields. The two-week ceasefire, which has been accepted by Iran and Israel, is intended to allow for the safe passage of oil through the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route.

items.title: Hurricanes edge Bruins in OT to clinch division title
items.text_preview: Strait of Hormuz to open after last minute U.S.-Iran Deal Gunman killed in shooting outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul Nearly 110 deaths reported in Afghanistan from flooding, landslides Trump,...
items.summary: The Carolina Hurricanes clinched the Metropolitan Division title by defeating the Boston Bruins 6-5 in overtime. Jaccob Slavin scored the winning goal 1:13 into overtime, securing the division title for the Hurricanes in their home finale. The Hurricanes now have a 50-22-6 record with 106 points, while the Bruins have a 43-26-10 record with 96 points.
